topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, bright and unwelcome, on my phone screen. My stomach instantly clenched, a cold knot tightening beneath my ribs. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. My breath hitched, and I fumbled for my phone, my fingers clumsy and slick with sudden anxiety. I stared at the screen, the words blurring for a second before I forced myself to read them: "Great presentation today, Sarah. Hope you're enjoying the break!" A wave of intense heat rushed up my neck. I quickly minimized the app, my cheeks burning as I tried to look intensely interested in my textbook, pretending the entire event hadn't just caused a complete internal system failure.

[story 2]

I was halfway through my lunch, trying to focus on the lukewarm sandwich in front of me, when my phone vibrated violently on the desk. It was a new follower notification. My eyes darted toward the screen, and the world seemed to narrow down to that tiny rectangle of glass. It was Mr. Harrison’s profile picture, smiling broadly. A strange, dizzying sensation washed over me, like the floor tilting. I felt my hands start to tremble uncontrollably, and I gripped the edge of the table so hard my knuckles turned white. I desperately wished the ground would swallow me whole.

[story 3]

It happened during the evening scroll. I was just casually looking through my feed when I saw it—a post from Mr. Harrison tagging me in a photo from the school event. My jaw went slack. I dropped my phone onto my lap with a soft clatter that sounded deafening in the quiet room. I sat back, staring blankly at the wall, my entire body rigid. Every muscle felt suddenly tense, coiled tight. I could feel the flush creeping up my neck and across my face, a hot, undeniable spreading sensation. I squeezed my eyes shut for a long moment, trying to force the strange, unwelcome feeling to dissipate.
